codeReview.summary: The attachment-lineage logic itself remains sound, but the associated-files baseline change hard-codes a nonzero linked-file count and therefore misrepresents availability before any component-specific data is loaded.

issues.description: `linkedFileCount` is derived from the static placeholder catalog, so the new summary always reports linked files as present (`已关联 12 个文件`) even on a fresh designer form with no associated-file lineage. This violates the feature contract that the area stay explainable as a baseline placeholder until real data is available.

The new M1 baseline summary is only covered by pure helper tests; there is no component-level assertion proving `AssociatedFilesList` or `InitiateReviewPanel` renders the placeholder state versus the linked-files state, so this regression slipped through despite the new helper coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `review-initiation-worker` skill requires live browser verification on `http://127.0.0.1:3101/`, but the worker handoff still returned success after explicitly skipping that step. The procedure and success criteria are not aligned.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/review-initiation-worker/SKILL.md step 9 requires manual verification; handoff `2026-03-13T14-38-45-407Z__m1-attachment-lineage-and-associated-files__444fa45a-4c6a-4d97-a600-434afdd5fd19.json` records `followedProcedure: false` and `whatWasLeftUndone` says browser validation was not performed.

summary: Fail: the associated-files summary now claims linked files exist based on static placeholder data, which makes the designer baseline misleading before real associated-file lineage is available; helper tests were added, but no component-level regression test caught the rendered-state mismatch.
